original_source:
"""
The purpose of this snippet is to test your knowledge of
default arguments for functions in python and how they
can be misused
"""


def foo(bar=[]):
    bar.append("baz")
    return bar
failing_expectation: Two calls to foo() should each return ["baz"], but the mutable default list makes the second call return ["baz", "baz"].
expected_solution: Suggest changing the signature to foo(bar=None) and creating a new list inside the function when bar is None. Do not modify source during the token comparison.
